vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine

verify.rule: All required fields and features are present.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to high heat generation.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling systems or optimize material selection.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure at spar_socket under extreme wing loading.
risk.mit: Conduct extensive load testing and reinforce if necessary.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for GPS boom installation.
risk.mit: Review and adjust GPS boom mount dimensions.

risk.desc: Potential interference between belly camera window and landing gear.
risk.mit: Reposition belly camera window or landing gear mount.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical short due to inadequate harness routing.
risk.mit: Ensure proper harness routing and insulation.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Structural backbone, avionics housing, and mounting point for wing spars and sensors

physics_paths.description: Transfers wing loading into the fuselage
Power and data lines to GPS boom and belly camera

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Optimize avionics bay layout for heat management
Enhance fuselage spine stiffness for higher wing loading

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.5mm for critical fits, ±1mm for non-critical features
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for overhangs during FDM, orientation for minimal layer lines
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for surface finish, CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.focus: Load testing of spar_socket under simulated wing loading
Thermal cycling of avionics bay to ensure heat dissipation
Electrical continuity test for GPS boom and belly camera wiring

risk.desc: Potential stress concentration at wing spar socket due to sharp edges.
risk.mit: Add radius to edges and chamfer corners.

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may not provide sufficient clearance for full extension.
risk.mit: Increase size of landing gear mount and adjust geometry.

risk.desc: Service access cover might interfere with wing spar during installation.
risk.mit: Adjust position or size of service access cover.

risk.mit: Use higher strength material or add reinforcement.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance around nacelle tilt mechanism may restrict movement.
risk.mit: Increase clearance around nacelle tilt mechanism.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure

internal_feature_targets.description: Dual 15mm CFRP spar running along the wing's centerline.
Socket for attaching the wing spar to the fuselage spine.
Attachment point for the fuselage spine.
Attachment point for the V-tail structure.
Attachment point for the ruddervator structure.
Attachment point for the stabilizer structure.
Mounting point for the landing gear.
Support structure for the landing gear leg.
Cover providing access for maintenance and replacement of motor/servo modules.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Nacelle interface datums, harness routing through tilt axis, hatches, connection types and load-path closure

physics_paths.path_description: Electrical signals from nacelle electronics pass through the harness_channel to the wing structure.
Heat from nacelle components dissipates through the service_hatch and surrounding CFRP to ambient air.
Mechanical loads from the nacelle are transferred through the interface_datum to the wing structure.

integration_opportunities.description: Service hatch provides easy access for maintenance and inspection.
Harness channel allows for flexible harness routing and strain relief.
Multiple fastener patterns ensure secure attachment and load distribution.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the subsystem mass does not exceed the budgeted 500g.
Verify the load path closure can handle the specified mechanical loads.
Check the harness_channel and connector_keying for proper electrical continuity.
